g.text: Bit 7 (MSB) | Continuation flag | 1 = more bytes follow, 0 = last byte
Bits 6..0 | Data payload | 7 bits of actual integer value per byte
Byte 9 (special) | Full 8 bits | No continuation flag needed (max reached)
Total capacity | 7×8 + 8 = 64 bits | Encodes full uint64_t
Bytes 0-7 | 1xxxxxxx each | All have continuation bit set
Byte 8 | xxxxxxxx | Full 8 bits, no continuation flag
uint64_t | 8 zero bytes + 0x2A | 8 bytes (7 bytes wasted)
System | Bit Order | Continuation | Max Bytes | Max Bits
Protocol Buffers (LEB128) | Little-endian (LSB first) | Bit 7 = more | 10 | 64
DWARF debug info (ULEB128) | Little-endian (LSB first) | Bit 7 = more | variable | unbounded
Byte order | Big-endian | High bits in first byte
9th byte rule | All 8 bits are data | No continuation flag
Signed values | Two's complement in value bits | Negative via zigzag or direct
Used for | Record headers, payload sizes, rowids | Throughout page format
Byte order | Little-endian | Low bits in first byte
Decode | Strip MSB, concatenate LSB-first | Reverse of SQLite order
Used for | Field tags, string lengths, int32/int64 | Wire format serialization
Key difference | Leading 1-count encodes length | Self-synchronizing (any byte reveals position)
String length = 5 | 8 bytes | 1 byte | 7 bytes saved
Serial type = 1 (int8) | 8 bytes | 1 byte | 7 bytes saved
Per-row overhead (4 varints) | 32 bytes | ~5 bytes | ~27 bytes saved
50 rows per page | 1,600 bytes overhead | ~250 bytes | ~1,350 bytes reclaimed
Reclaimed space | - | ~19 extra rows per page | Higher B-tree fanout
Encoding | ~3-5 ns per varint | Loop + shift + mask
Decoding | ~2-4 ns per varint | Loop + shift + OR
Disk read (4KB page) | ~100,000 ns (SSD) | 25,000x more expensive
Verdict | CPU cost is negligible vs I/O | Always worth it for storage
Quick Reference: Value Range to Byte Count
